items.briefing:
Security researchers are warning of a shift in how attackers compromise Microsoft 365 accounts, with stealthier methods making these attacks harder to detect — a concern for any Australian organisation relying on Microsoft's platform for email and collaboration. Separately, the FBI has issued a warning about fake FIFA websites designed to steal money and personal information ahead of the World Cup, which is relevant to anyone in Australia purchasing tickets or merchandise online.

On the horizon, experts are also flagging the growing risk of "Q Day" — when quantum computing could break current encryption — urging organisations to start planning now rather than waiting.

**Practical tip:** If you're buying FIFA World Cup tickets or merchandise, only use the official FIFA website and be cautious of deals shared via social media or email links.

featured.title: Certes says be ready to protect data before Q Day hits
featured.quote: Only 3% of Australian businesses prepared for post-quantum cryptography threats to sensitive data.
